title: NVIDIA NeMo Automodel scales Diffusers fine-tuning.
summary: Hugging Face and NVIDIA published an official workflow for fine-tuning image and video generation models at scale using NVIDIA NeMo Automodel together with the Hugging Face Diffusers library. The post focuses on adapting pretrained diffusion-based models rather than introducing a new model architecture. Fine-tuning large image and video generators can be expensive and operationally complex, so a scalable workflow is useful for teams adapting generative models to specific styles, domains, or product requirements. The collaboration also signals tighter integration between Hugging Face’s model ecosystem and NVIDIA’s GPU-oriented training stack. NeMo Automodel is described as an open training library under the NVIDIA NeMo framework that targets scalable training and fine-tuning with PyTorch DTensor-native SPMD techniques. Diffusers provides pretrained diffusion models for image, video, and audio generation, so the workflow is most relevant to teams already building on the Hugging Face and PyTorch ecosystem.
what_happened: Hugging Face and NVIDIA published an official workflow for fine-tuning image and video generation models at scale using NVIDIA NeMo Automodel together with the Hugging Face Diffusers library. The post focuses on adapting pretrained diffusion-based models rather than introducing a new model architecture. Fine-tuning large image and video generators can be expensive and operationally complex, so a scalable workflow is useful for teams adapting generative models to specific styles, domains, or product requirements. The collaboration also signals tighter integration between Hugging Face’s model ecosystem and NVIDIA’s GPU-oriented training stack. NeMo Automodel is described as an open training library under the NVIDIA NeMo framework that targets scalable training and fine-tuning with PyTorch DTensor-native SPMD techniques. Diffusers provides pretrained diffusion models for image, video, and audio generation, so the workflow is most relevant to teams already building on the Hugging Face and PyTorch ecosystem.
why_it_matters: Diffusion models are generative models commonly used to create images, videos, and audio by learning how to reverse a noise-adding process. Fine-tuning means taking a pretrained model and continuing training on new data so its outputs better match a target domain, style, or application. Hugging Face Diffusers is a PyTorch library that packages many state-of-the-art diffusion pipelines, while NVIDIA NeMo Automodel is positioned as a scalable training and fine-tuning layer for generative AI workloads on NVIDIA GPUs.

Fine-tuning means taking a pretrained model and continuing training on new data so its outputs better match a target domain, style, or application. Hugging Face Diffusers is a PyTorch library that packages many state-of-the-art diffusion pipelines, while NVIDIA NeMo Automodel is positioned as a scalable training and fine-tuning layer for generative AI workloads on NVIDIA GPUs.
actions: Check whether the team has a near-term pilot scenario for diffusion-models, fine-tuning.
